trades.market: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP during Q4 2026 through Q4 2027
trades.catalyst: The July 30 Q2 2026 advance GDP estimate will update economic momentum before the contract's measurement window begins; subsequent quarterly BEA releases are the decisive catalysts.
trades.risk_notes: Persistently restrictive Fed policy, an energy shock, or synchronized contraction could produce consecutive negative quarters. The contract uses a mechanical GDP definition rather than an NBER recession designation, and downside outcomes are highly correlated across quarters.
trades.grounding_source: BEA Gross Domestic Product data: https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product; historical quarterly real-GDP growth series: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/A191RL1Q225SBEA

avoid.reason: The zero-cut and at-least-one-cut prices are nearly complementary, while their measurement windows differ. The supplied data do not support an independent edge of at least 8%.

avoid.market: July 2026 CPI above 3.4% year over year
avoid.reason: The inflationary regime raises upside risk, but the related monthly CPI pricing signals weak July inflation. Without contemporaneous CPI components, the estimated edge is not robust enough.
The threshold ladder implies a tightly centered growth distribution, and no credible deviation of at least 8% from the 30.5% implied probability is supported.
They fail the required minimum $50,000 volume filter.

notes: Market pricing indicates an inflationary regime: rate cuts before 2027 are assigned low probability, inflation remains elevated, and energy and precious-metal contracts reflect commodity pressure. Growth pricing remains positive, making a 42.5% probability of consecutive negative-GDP quarters in the specified 2027 window appear elevated relative to historical frequency.
